committee.name: Operations Manager
interests: Whether the Manager Review Queue surfaces urgent requests prominently so critical purchases never stall on her desk
How PO.vin handles delegation when she's traveling — specifically whether she can hand her queue to a peer manager without losing visibility
Whether the approval threshold bands can be tuned so routine low-dollar purchases skip her stage and hit her queue only when warranted
Whether escalation from manager stage to owner is one click or requires explanation fields that slow her down
The clarification cycle — how quickly a returned PO reaches the requester and comes back to her for re-review
Whether holds are visible to requesters so they stop asking why their PO hasn't moved
How the Manager Dashboard shows aged or overdue items at a glance, not just a raw count
Whether she can review a PO on mobile when she's on-site, not only at her desk
That separation-of-duties enforcement is automatic and she isn't responsible for policing who approves whose requests
Whether departments are free-text so she doesn't have to file an admin ticket every time a new cost center is created
How rejected POs are communicated — whether the requester sees a reason or just a status flip
That the activity timeline on the PO detail shows exactly who acted and when, not a condensed summary
decisionCriteria: Her review queue must show urgent-flagged items at the top — if urgent requests can be buried by newer submissions she won't trust it
Delegation must preserve her visibility: she needs to see what she delegated and the outcome, not just hand it off and lose track
Clarification round-trips must be trackable — she needs to know when a clarification was sent, when the requester responded, and re-enter the flow without hunting for the PO
The threshold engine must work without her manually checking every PO's dollar amount — if routing is not automatic it adds work, not savings
Hold and resume must have an audit row — if she holds a PO for a budget question, that conversation must be on the record
Escalation to Owner stage must not require her to re-enter PO data already entered by the requester
The Activity tab must show every state transition, not just approvals, so she can explain to a requester exactly where their PO is
She needs per-department spend visibility in Reports to justify whether her team's requests are on-budget for the quarter
PO.vin must route by dollar amount automatically — she should never have to decide where to send a request, only whether to approve it
Rejection must require a reason field that lands in the audit trail — she can't defend a rejection that has no documented rationale
System must enforce that she cannot approve a PO she herself prepared — SOD must be automatic, not a policy reminder
CSV export from the Registry must include department, status, and amount so she can analyze her team's request patterns outside PO.vin
goals: Keep her manager review queue cleared within one business day so requests don't bottleneck at her stage
Never have a purchase stall because she was unavailable — delegate cleanly and resume her queue without gaps
Ensure every department request goes through a documented approval, not informal email chains that leave no audit trail
Use the threshold configuration to auto-skip her stage for petty-cash-level purchases so her queue holds only meaningful decisions
Reduce requester follow-up messages by making hold and clarification reasons visible on the PO itself
Produce a quarterly snapshot of her team's approved spend by budget category without needing to pull raw data from accounting
Give new managers on her team a clear queue-based workflow so onboarding into the approval process takes minutes, not a training session
Ensure urgent purchases are visible on her dashboard the moment they're submitted so she can triage immediately
Have documentation for every rejected request so HR or compliance can review rationale if questioned
Reduce the number of verbal purchase approvals that bypass the system — every dollar goes through PO.vin
Use the activity timeline on the PO detail to self-serve answers to requester status questions without needing to call accounting
objections: My team will just call me directly to approve things rather than learn a new queue — adoption depends on managers actually using it, not reverting to email
If the threshold bands are misconfigured at launch, I'll get flooded with low-value requests that should never reach me — who audits the band setup?
The delegate feature sounds useful, but if the person I delegate to can't find my queue or doesn't know the context, I've just moved the bottleneck

I'm worried about requesters submitting duplicates because they don't trust the status — the duplicate-vendor banner on the wizard helps, but what about repeat requests to the same vendor for different line items?
If a requester submits without a GL account on the lines, and it reaches me before accounting catches it, I'm approving something that won't finalize cleanly
The Reports page shows quarter-to-date and 90-day views — if I need a custom date range for a budget conversation, I can't get it
Clarification returns a PO to the requester — but what if they don't log back in promptly? I have no way to escalate a stalled clarification
My staff turnover is higher than average — re-assigning manager relationships in Manage Users every time someone leaves is an admin overhead I didn't budget for
Hold is on the PO detail, not the queue — if I want to hold six POs during a budget freeze I have to open each one individually rather than bulk-act from the queue
I don't see a way to see my team's total requested value for the month before I start approving — I'm making approval decisions without knowing how close we are to a budget ceiling
openQuestions: When I open my Manager Review Queue, are urgent-flagged POs guaranteed to be at the top, or does the sort reset if I reload the page?
If I delegate a PO to a peer manager, does it disappear from my queue entirely, or do I keep a read-only view of its progress?
How does the system handle it if the peer I delegated to is also out — can delegation chain, and is there a fallback to me or to the Owner?
When I put a PO on hold, does the requester see the hold reason on their My Requests page, or only that the status changed?
Can the threshold bands be set so that anything under a configurable dollar amount skips my stage entirely, and if so, where do I see a list of what was auto-bypassed?
If a requester submits a PO with a blank GL account on one of the lines, does that fail validation at submit, or does it pass through to my queue and only fail at finalize?
Can I approve a PO with conditions from the Manager stage, or is approve-with-conditions limited to the Owner stage?
How long does the Activity tab retain events — if I'm reviewing an old dispute, can I see state changes from six months ago?
If I reject a PO, is the requester notified immediately, and does the rejection reason appear in the notification or only on the PO detail?
Is there a way to bulk-approve low-risk POs from the queue list without opening each PO detail individually?
When a clarification is returned by the requester, do I receive a notification, or do I need to check my queue manually?
The Reports page shows Avg. Decision Time — is that calculated per manager so I can benchmark my team's throughput, or is it org-wide only?
If one of my direct reports is re-assigned to a different manager in Manage Users, do their in-flight POs in my queue stay with me or transfer?
Does the CSV export from the Registry include the requester name and budget category, or do I need to pull the full audit bundle per PO to get that?
If I escalate a PO to the Owner stage, can I still see it in my queue for visibility, or does it drop off entirely once I escalate?

committee.name: Purchasing Manager
interests: The PO Wizard's five-step flow and whether it captures everything purchasing needs — vendor, department, line items with classification, GL account, and business justification — without requiring a second form
The vendor directory and whether it enforces use of approved suppliers rather than letting requesters type in any vendor name
Approval threshold configuration and whether the dollar-band routing rules reflect the organization's actual authorization matrix
Whether the duplicate-vendor banner on the wizard catches repeat orders to the same supplier before they're submitted, not after
The audit trail on each PO — specifically that every approval, hold, clarification, delegation, and override is timestamped and role-attributed in the Activity tab
Spend reports by vendor, department, and budget category, and whether they can be exported for monthly procurement reviews
Whether the system enforces a GL account on every line before a PO can be finalized, so nothing closes with an unclassified cost
The separation-of-duties controls — that the person who prepares a PO cannot approve it, and the person who starts a payment cannot mark it paid
How open-PO aging is tracked so he can see what's been approved but not yet paid or received, and for how long
Whether vendor risk classification is computed automatically and whether he can override it when the system gets it wrong
How the receive queue and per-line receipt process captures partial deliveries and ties them back to the original PO lines
That every workflow action — approve, reject, hold, delegate, escalate — is available on the PO detail without needing a separate tool
decisionCriteria: PO.vin must cover the entire purchase lifecycle — request → approval → payment → receipt → close — in one system, not two linked tools with a gap in the middle
Threshold bands must be editable by an admin without a software release so he can adjust authorization limits when the business changes them
The vendor directory must be the single source for supplier selection — requesters must not be able to commit spend to an unlisted supplier without admin intervention
Every governed action must write to the audit trail automatically, not as an optional note — he needs to be able to produce a complete history for any PO on demand
The open-PO aging report must show time-in-stage so he can identify approvals sitting past SLA, not just the current status
GL account must be required at finalize, not at submit, so requesters aren't blocked early, but nothing closes without a coded cost
Separation-of-duties enforcement must be automatic and not rely on managers remembering a policy
The line-item classification options — asset, consumable, service, software, subscription — must match the chart of accounts categories used in the GL
He needs to be able to export the Registry with filters (department, status, date range, amount) so monthly procurement reporting doesn't require manual data assembly
The asset registry must auto-populate from received asset lines — he cannot manage equipment records separately from the PO that created them
Notification rules must be configurable so the right people are alerted at the right stage without email overload for everyone
The system must handle clarification cycles cleanly — a returned PO must restate the clarification request to the requester without losing the original submission data
goals: Be the internal owner of PO.vin configuration — thresholds, notification rules, vendor directory, GL accounts — so he doesn't depend on a vendor or IT team for operational changes
Eliminate purchase requests that arrive via email or verbal approval and never get recorded — every dollar of spend goes through PO.vin before it's committed
Produce a monthly vendor spend summary from the Reports page without manually reconciling spreadsheets
Ensure no PO is finalized with a blank GL account — GL coding must be complete before a purchase closes
Track which POs are aging in each stage so he can follow up with slow approvers before month-end
Build a vendor directory that reflects the approved supplier list, not whatever names requesters type into the wizard
Reduce duplicate vendor orders by surfacing the duplicate-vendor banner prominently enough that requesters catch their own repeats at submission
Maintain a complete audit trail for every PO so procurement decisions can be reviewed months later without reconstructing from email
Establish clear spend thresholds that route correctly — petty amounts handled at manager stage, capital and high-value purchases escalated to owner — and tune them over time
Own the bulk-import process so that launching PO.vin with an existing vendor list, department list, and user roster doesn't require manual data entry
Become the go-to resource for other departments when they have PO.vin questions — champion adoption by knowing every screen and workflow
objections: If the vendor directory is non-mandatory — if requesters can type a vendor name that doesn't exist in the directory — the approved-supplier benefit disappears entirely and I'm back to chasing unapproved vendors after the fact

Threshold bands apply to new submissions only; any in-flight POs at go-live stay on the old routing — I need a plan for migrating or closing out everything that was approved via our old process before I can declare the system of record

The bulk CSV importer has six types and a dry-run pattern, which is good, but I've been burned before by imports that committed successfully and then couldn't be rolled back — I need to know exactly what 'deactivate manually' looks like for a 400-row user import gone wrong

The notification rules are on/off toggles for seeded event keys, with no way to add new events from the UI — if I need a rule for a workflow event that isn't in the default seed, I'm stuck until the next software update
The PO Wizard auto-saves drafts, which is helpful, but I need to confirm that a draft from a terminated employee doesn't persist as orphaned data that my team then can't discard without admin access

The Reports page shows spend by quarter or last 90 days — those are pre-set windows, and my budget cycle runs April–March, not calendar quarters, so the reports may not map cleanly to my reporting obligations
Open-PO aging is a downloadable CSV, not a live dashboard tile — if I need to check aging during a meeting I have to download and open a file rather than see the numbers inline

Vendor risk classification is computed automatically, and I can override the classification by toggling 'Override active', but there's no field for a reason or note on the override — I lose the rationale the next time someone asks why a high-risk vendor was cleared

The help editor stores draft overrides per-browser, not in the cloud, which means if I update the help content on my laptop it won't be visible to the team until I export and apply it to the next release — that's a deployment dependency I wasn't expecting

There's no mention of a supplier onboarding or new-vendor request workflow — if a requester wants to use a vendor not in the directory, the current path appears to be type a new name in the wizard, which bypasses the directory entirely
openQuestions: On the PO Wizard, if a requester selects a vendor not in the directory — typing a new name in the type-ahead — does that create a pending vendor request for admin review, or does it go straight through?
Can I set a threshold band that routes any PO over a certain dollar amount to Owner stage regardless of which department submitted it, or do thresholds apply per department?
When a PO is finalized — all lines have GL accounts, goods are received, status moves to Closed — where does finalization show in the audit trail and who can trigger it?
The bulk CSV importer requires zero errored rows to commit — if 3 of 200 rows have errors, do the clean 197 rows commit or does the entire import fail?
When I archive a GL account, do in-flight POs that reference it break at finalize, or does the system allow finalization against an archived account if the line was created before archiving?
The Vendor Spend CSV is a specialized report — does it break spend down by invoice month, by PO submission date, or by the date the PO was paid?
If I need to add a new approval threshold band for a mid-year policy change, how quickly does it take effect — does the next submitted PO get the new routing immediately?

The separation-of-duties rule prevents me from approving a PO I prepared — but I'm both the Purchasing Manager and the Admin. If I set up PO.vin in admin mode and also sit in the approval chain, will the SOD gate block my approvals for POs I created administratively?
When a PO is put on hold and then resumed, does it restart the SLA clock or does aging continue from the original submission date?

The audit bundle CSV downloads the full history for one PO — if I need the audit trail for all POs from a vendor across the last year, do I have to download them one at a time or is there a cross-PO audit export?
Does the duplicate-vendor banner fire during the wizard at vendor selection, or only at submission? Can a requester dismiss it and submit anyway, and does that dismissal get recorded?
When I configure notification rules, can I specify which users receive each notification type, or does a notification type go to all users in the relevant role?
If I have a line item with classification 'asset' but the requester forgot to fill in the asset category, does the wizard catch that before submit or at finalize?
For the receive queue, when a partial receipt is recorded on one PO line, what status does the PO move to, and does it appear in the accounting queue again for the remaining lines?
The Locations Admin lets me archive a location — if I archive a location that's currently assigned to open PO lines or assets, do those lines and assets retain the location name in their records?

committee.name: Department Manager
interests: Whether her staff can submit purchase requests without needing to understand the approval routing — the wizard should handle routing automatically so she doesn't have to explain it to each new employee
Seeing her department's active and pending POs in one view without filtering through org-wide data that doesn't concern her
Whether the clarification cycle is fast enough that a returned PO doesn't hold up operations for days while the requester figures out what to fix
How requesters on her team track the status of their own submissions without pinging her directly for updates
Whether the urgency flag on the PO Wizard actually surfaces requests at the top of the manager queue so a genuinely time-sensitive purchase doesn't wait behind routine items
Whether she needs to log in just to approve a PO, or whether there's a quick approve flow from a notification email
That draft POs auto-save so her staff don't lose work if they get interrupted mid-wizard
How business justification is captured — whether a short justification field is sufficient or whether the 20-character minimum blocks quick routine purchases
Whether she can delegate approvals when she's on leave without creating a configuration change that persists after she's back
Whether the PO detail shows the full request including attachments, so she doesn't have to ask the requester to resend a quote before she can evaluate it
decisionCriteria: Her staff must be able to complete the PO Wizard in under ten minutes for a routine supply order — if it feels like a form project they'll find workarounds
The My Requests page must give requesters a clear status view so they stop asking her where their PO is
Clarification must include a visible reason so the requester knows exactly what to fix — a status change alone isn't enough
Urgency flag must demonstrably change queue position — if urgent items aren't prioritized she'll have no credibility telling staff to use it
The system must email her when a PO reaches her queue, because she doesn't intend to log in speculatively to check
Delegation must be time-limited or easily reversed — she doesn't want a temporary absence to permanently change approval routing
Attachment viewing must be available directly on the PO detail, not require a separate download step for scanned-clean files
Budget category field must map to categories her department actually uses — if the options are too generic she'll get miscoded requests
Rejected PO reason must be visible to the requester so she doesn't have to explain the rejection verbally after the fact
Draft auto-save must be reliable — if staff lose a partially filled wizard they'll stop trusting the system
goals: Ensure every purchase request from her department goes through PO.vin rather than informal channels that leave no record
Stop spending time as a status-relay between her staff and accounting — My Requests and PO detail should answer 'where is my PO?' without her involvement
Use the Reports page to pull her department's quarterly spend by budget category and share it with her manager in budget reviews
Delegate her queue cleanly when she's on leave so approvals don't stall — and reclaim it cleanly when she returns
Reduce the number of POs that come back via clarification because of missing or insufficient business justification — coaching her team on the wizard once should be enough
Have a clear record of every rejected request so she can revisit recurring rejections and address the underlying procurement issue
Keep her team's vendor relationships consistent by using the vendor directory instead of letting every requester bring in a different supplier for the same product
Use the duplicate-vendor banner to catch double orders before they're submitted, not after accounting flags them
Make sure urgent purchases for client-facing work are visible to approvers immediately — same-day approval when genuinely needed
Build her team's muscle for self-sufficient procurement so she's out of the loop on routine purchases and only involved for higher-value decisions
objections: My staff has high turnover in junior roles — the time investment in training new employees on the PO Wizard is real, and if the wizard is long or confusing, they'll ask me to submit it for them, which defeats the point

The 20-character minimum on business justification sounds minor but will cause confusion for routine purchases like 'reorder printer paper' — is there a way to use saved justification templates for recurring purchase types?
I'm skeptical the urgency flag will be respected if managers have 20 items in their queue — urgent loses meaning if overused, and there's no policy on what qualifies as urgent

If I'm listed as the manager for my direct reports in the system, I'm now a required approval step for everything they submit — I didn't expect to be in the formal approval chain, I expected to be a supervisor who gets visibility
My staff will want to know the status of their PO in real time — if the only place to see it is the My Requests page and they have to log in to check, they'll ask me instead
Delegation is useful when I'm traveling, but if I have to ask an admin to set it up every time I leave, the overhead is too high for short trips

I don't have a reporting role beyond manager — I can see my department's requests in the Registry filtered by department, but I can't see a spend-against-budget comparison without doing the math manually
The clarification cycle sends the PO back to the requester, but if the requester is on vacation, the clarification can sit for days and I have no way to reassign the response obligation
If I delegate approvals while I’m out, can I still see what was approved in my name when I’m back?
Will my team be forced to fill in fields that don’t apply to our purchases just to submit a request?
openQuestions: When one of my staff submits a PO, do I receive an email notification at that moment, or only when something requires my action in the approval queue?
On My Requests, can my staff see the current approval stage — like 'Pending Manager' versus 'Pending Owner' — or only a generic 'under review' status?
If my team submits a PO with a vendor not in the vendor directory, does the wizard allow that or block submission until an admin adds the vendor?
When I send a PO back via clarification, does the requester see my clarification comment on the PO detail, or only in the email notification?
Is the urgency flag something the requester sets, or can I as the approving manager escalate a PO to urgent after it reaches my queue?
If I'm on leave and I haven't delegated my queue, what happens to POs that arrive — do they just sit until I return, or is there an automatic escalation after a set number of days?
Can a requester on my team attach a PDF quote from a vendor and have it be accessible on the PO detail, or are attachments only visible to the admin and accounting roles?

If a PO is submitted by my direct report but has an error on one line — wrong quantity, for example — can I fix it as the approving manager, or do I have to send it back via clarification for the requester to correct?
The Reports page shows spend by department — is 'department' pulled from the Department field the requester enters in the wizard, or from the department in their user profile?
If I want to see all POs for my department that are currently on hold, is there a filter in the Registry for 'On Hold' status, or do I need to export and filter the CSV?
When a PO is rejected, does the rejection reason appear on the requester's My Requests page next to the PO, or do they have to open the PO detail and read the Activity tab to find it?
Can a requester resubmit a rejected PO, or does a rejection require them to start a new request from scratch in the wizard?
Is there a way for me to see all POs for my department that are currently pending payment or awaiting receipt, or does that view require an accounting role?
If I need to split a large purchase across two budget categories on separate lines, does the PO Wizard support multi-line POs with different categories per line, or is it one category per PO?
When the threshold engine bypasses a stage for a low-dollar PO, does my staff's PO go straight to approved, and do they get notified of that, or does it look like it's still waiting?

committee.name: Site Manager
interests: Whether POs can be tagged by location so he can see purchasing activity specific to his site without wading through requests from other branches

How the receive queue and per-line receipt process maps to physical delivery at his site — he's often the person who physically checks in goods and needs the process to match what actually happens on the ground
Whether the asset registry tracks physical location per asset so he knows what equipment is assigned to his site versus the main office
Whether urgent-flagged POs for site-critical repairs or consumables get approved fast enough to prevent operational downtime
How the PO Wizard handles maintenance and facilities purchases — consumables, contracted services, replacement parts — without requiring accounting knowledge he doesn't have
Whether he can see the status of site-related POs across all departments at his location, or only requests he personally submitted
That the approval routing doesn't add days to purchases that are already time-sensitive because a piece of equipment is down
How on-hold POs are communicated back to him so he knows the reason and can act on it without chasing the approver
Whether inventory records for consumables at his site reflect actual stock, so he's not ordering items that are already on hand
That POs for contracted vendors his site uses regularly are easy to raise without re-entering the same supplier information every time
decisionCriteria: Location tagging on PO lines must be granular enough that site-level reporting is meaningful — if all locations roll up to 'main' his site is invisible in the data
The receive process must accommodate partial deliveries — facilities orders often arrive in batches and he can't always wait for a complete delivery to confirm receipt
Asset records must carry location and assignment so when equipment is transferred between sites the history follows the asset, not the building
The urgency flag must be respected in the approval queue — if flagging a request as urgent doesn't measurably accelerate approval he'll stop using it

Business justification requirements must be practical for facilities use cases — 'replace broken HVAC filter' is self-evident, but the 20-character minimum shouldn't create a bureaucratic hurdle for obvious operational needs
Vendor directory must include the maintenance and service contractors his site uses regularly, not just the preferred suppliers from corporate procurement
He must be able to see at least the status of POs he submitted on My Requests without needing an accounting or admin role to see what accounting has done with them
Inventory levels for site consumables must reflect PO receipts in near real time so he doesn't order supplies that are already in receiving
On-hold reason must be visible on the PO detail — he's not going to check the Activity tab for context, he needs the hold reason somewhere obvious
Approval turnaround must support his site's operational rhythm — a 48-hour approval window is acceptable for planned purchases but not for emergency repairs
goals: Have a clear record of all approved and in-flight purchasing activity for his site so he can manage supplier relationships and delivery expectations
Eliminate the practice of making site purchases on a personal card and expensing them later — PO.vin should make the legitimate procurement channel fast enough to use even for moderately urgent needs
Use the inventory module to track consumable stock at his site so reorder decisions are based on actual on-hand levels, not gut feel
Ensure every piece of equipment at his site has an asset record with accurate location and custodian so he can account for it at year-end
Reduce the number of purchase requests that come back via clarification because he didn't know what budget category or GL account to choose — onboarding should cover the common options for facilities
Get visibility into which site vendors are classified as high-risk so he can raise concerns before a critical purchase is flagged and delayed
Have a documented approval trail for every maintenance contract renewal and capital repair so audits don't become a scramble to find paper records
Use the duplicate-vendor banner to catch his team from re-ordering from the same supplier twice in the same week for the same consumable
Build a short list of approved site vendors in the directory so requesters default to known suppliers rather than finding new ones for every purchase
Keep my site’s purchasing moving even when the head-office approver is slow, without breaking the spend rules.
objections: Location is a field on the line item, not on the PO header — if I have a mixed PO with lines going to different areas on my site, filtering by location in the Registry won't isolate it cleanly

The receive queue is an accounting-role screen — if I'm the person physically checking in deliveries at the site, I have to relay receipt confirmation to accounting rather than recording it directly, which adds a step and a delay

I'm listed as a manager role for approval routing, but my actual job is running the site, not approving procurement for every department that happens to report through me — I'll be approving requests I don't have context on

The vendor directory risk classification is computed automatically but I don't know what signals it uses — if a local contractor I've worked with for years gets flagged as high-risk because they're small, I need a way to explain the override

Asset records are created at receipt, but the physical asset tag number often isn't assigned until a week later when my team gets around to labeling it — if the serial number and asset tag aren't entered at receive time, they may never be entered

There's no mention of a location-level spend budget that I can see — I can filter the Reports by department, but if my site cuts across multiple departments, I can't see site-level spend without combining multiple department exports manually

The clarification cycle is efficient when the requester is at a desk and checks email, but half of my site workforce is mobile and doesn't monitor their work email continuously — clarification response times will be slow
Our site has spotty connectivity — can staff still raise and queue a PO when the network drops?
If head office changes the approval routing, will it silently reroute my site’s requests without telling me?
Can I see every open PO for my site in one place, or do I have to chase each one through someone else’s queue?
openQuestions: When I filter the Purchase Registry by location, does the filter match the location field on PO lines, or does it match a site-level attribute on the PO header?
If I'm the approving manager for my site team, can I see all POs awaiting my approval across all departments that report to me in one combined queue view?
When a PO is received partially — say two of four items arrived — does the partially received status show on the PO detail so I can confirm what's been checked in versus what's still outstanding?
Can I record the physical delivery and receipt directly from My Requests on my own submitted POs, or does receipt intake have to go through an accounting user?
Is the Location field on PO lines a free-text field or a dropdown from the Locations Admin table? If it's a dropdown, can I request new locations to be added, or does that require admin access?

When an asset is transferred between sites — say a laptop moves from my site to the main office — does the transfer action on the Asset Detail update the location automatically, and who can initiate that?

The inventory module tracks consumables by on-hand and available quantities — is there a way to associate an inventory item with a specific site so I see only my site's stock, not the org-wide combined quantity?
If a PO for site maintenance is flagged as urgent, what is the expected approval turnaround — is there an SLA target, and is it visible to me anywhere?

When the duplicate-vendor banner fires on the wizard, does it show me the prior PO to that vendor — including its status — so I can decide whether the new request is actually a duplicate or a separate legitimate order?
The vendor directory shows risk level but not the date it was last updated — if a vendor's risk classification was set two years ago, how do I know if it's current?
If I need to attach a delivery receipt or a photo of damaged goods at time of receipt, where does that attachment live — on the PO detail, or on the asset record, or both?
Can I see an aging view of POs that are still open for my site — approved but not yet paid, or paid but not yet received — without downloading the open-PO aging CSV and filtering it manually?
When a PO is closed, is the closure visible to me on My Requests, or do closed POs move to the Closed POs archive and disappear from my active view?

If I need to escalate an approval because my approver is unavailable and a repair is urgent, is escalation something I do as the requester, or does it require the approver to act on the PO themselves?
The Reports page has an equipment received stat tile — does 'equipment received' mean received asset-line POs specifically, and is it scoped to my site or org-wide?

committee.name: Administration Manager
interests: Whether she can manage the full Admin Settings without needing developer access — threshold bands, notification rules, user accounts, and GL accounts all in one place
The Manage Hub Users screen and how efficiently she can onboard new staff, update their manager assignments, and deactivate leavers as part of HR offboarding
Whether the bulk CSV importer supports all six data types — vendors, departments, GL accounts, locations, users, inventory items — and whether the dry-run pattern catches errors before committing
How notification rule configuration works and whether she can test-fire notifications without waiting for a real workflow event to verify email is working
The Help Editor and whether she can update in-app guidance for new workflows or policy changes without a software deployment
Whether the Integration Keys screen gives her enough information to diagnose a broken email notification — verified status, last-check result — without filing a support ticket
How the role-preview feature on the Admin Dashboard helps her test the system from an employee or manager perspective before rolling it out to staff
That user deactivation — not deletion — is the correct offboarding path and that deactivated users' historical POs and audit records remain intact
Whether the approval threshold bands section clearly explains that changes apply to new submissions only, so she doesn't get blamed when an in-flight PO routes on the old rules
That the admin role gives her visibility across all POs in the Registry, not just her own, so she can investigate and resolve user questions
Whether GL account archiving is safe for accounts that have been used on closed POs — she doesn't want to archive an account and find that historical reports break
How the physical locations table ties into both PO lines and asset records so location data is consistent org-wide and she manages it in one place
decisionCriteria: She must be able to configure approval thresholds, notification rules, and user management entirely from the Admin Settings and Manage Hub Users screens without any backend access
The bulk importer's dry-run must surface every row-level error before committing — she can't fix a partial import that's already been applied
User deactivation must be immediate and block login right away — offboarding a terminated employee cannot wait for a nightly sync
Notification test-fire must confirm email delivery, not just that the rule is toggled on — she needs to know the email provider integration is working
The role-preview feature must let her see exactly what an employee or manager sees without creating a test account — a single admin toggle is the right UX
GL account archiving must be safe to use on accounts that have historical PO references — finalized POs must retain the account name even if the account is archived
Manager assignment on user profiles must be the only configuration needed to populate manager queues — she should not need to touch threshold bands to make a new manager's queue work
The integration keys screen must show the current verification status of each key so she can identify a broken email or virus-scan integration at a glance
Threshold band changes must apply immediately to new submissions without a software release or restart — she's the one making the business-directed configuration change
Help editor export/import must allow her to maintain a local copy of help content overrides and apply them after any software update that resets the defaults
goals: Own the complete admin configuration of PO.vin so the business doesn't depend on IT or a vendor to make operational changes like adding a department or updating a threshold
Run a clean HR offboarding process — deactivate the user in PO.vin at the same time she revokes other system access, confident that their PO history is preserved for audit
Use the bulk CSV importer to launch PO.vin with the existing vendor list, user roster, and GL chart of accounts without manual data entry
Maintain the help content so employees get accurate guidance that reflects any changes to approval policy or workflow configuration she's made
Be the first point of contact for PO.vin user questions — with admin access she can look up any PO, check any user's queue, and resolve issues without escalating
Keep the integration keys healthy — proactively check that email notifications, file scanning, and any connected services are verified and not silently failing
Ensure GL accounts are kept current — archiving accounts for closed cost centers while keeping historical PO references intact
Build and maintain the notification rule set so approvers are alerted promptly at each stage without being overwhelmed with emails for every minor status change
Use the role-preview feature to conduct internal UAT before any policy change goes live — she needs to see what requesters will see before she sends a training email

Track admin dashboard SLA compliance as a leading indicator — if SLA compliance drops she can identify which stage is creating bottlenecks before the Purchasing Manager or Operations Manager escalates
objections: The Help Editor stores overrides per-browser, not in a shared backend — every time there's a software update that resets the defaults, she has to re-apply her overrides from the export JSON, which is a manual step she'll forget

Notification rules are on/off toggles for fixed event keys with no ability to add new events from the UI — if the business needs an alert for a workflow event that isn't in the default seed, she's stuck until a software update

There's a PO numbering and security card in Admin Settings that the documentation marks as a non-functional UI mockup — having a non-functional section in an admin screen is confusing and will generate user questions she can't answer

The dry-run/commit pattern on the bulk importer requires zero errored rows to commit — if a large user import has even one row with an invalid role value, the entire import is blocked until she fixes the CSV and re-runs the dry-run

Deactivated users can't be deleted — which is correct for audit integrity — but she needs confidence that deactivating a former employee clears their access immediately and doesn't leave a session window open

The vendor risk classification is computed automatically and she can override it with an 'Override active' toggle, but there's no reason or note field on the override — she can't explain to the Purchasing Manager six months later why a vendor was overridden

Threshold band changes apply to new submissions only, which is technically correct but will generate user confusion when in-flight POs route on the old rules after she's announced a policy change — she needs a way to communicate this clearly
The integration keys screen doesn't indicate when a key was last rotated — if there's a compliance requirement to rotate keys on a schedule, she has no way to verify when the last rotation occurred
Password reset is admin-mediated — there's no self-service forgot-password flow — which means she'll regularly receive login-help requests, especially from staff who log in infrequently

The role options in user management are a fixed set (employee/manager/owner/accounting/admin) — if the business needs a read-only reporting role for department managers who don't approve but want spend visibility, that doesn't exist
openQuestions: When I deactivate a user in Manage Hub Users, is their session terminated immediately, or does their active session remain valid until it expires naturally?

If I run a bulk user import and all 200 rows are committed, but three employees had the wrong manager assigned — is there a way to bulk-correct manager assignments, or do I need to edit each user profile individually?
The Help Editor stores overrides per-browser — if I set overrides on my work laptop and then my browser cache is cleared, are the overrides lost, or is the export JSON the only backup?
On the Integration Keys screen, what does 'stored but verification failed' mean in practice — is the email service completely non-functional, or does it fall back to a default behavior?
The PO numbering and security card in Admin Settings is marked as a non-functional mockup — is that section going to be removed, or is it planned functionality? What do I tell staff who ask about it?
When I archive a GL account that has been used on finalized POs, does the Reports page and the audit bundle CSV still show the original account name, or does it show as 'archived' or blank?

The threshold bands say changes apply to new submissions only — is there a way to see the threshold configuration that was active when a specific PO was submitted, so I can explain routing decisions for in-flight POs?

The notification test-fire button sends a test email to me — if I want to confirm that another user in a specific role (like a manager) would receive a notification, is there a way to test for their role, or only for mine?
If I promote an employee to manager in their user profile, do their existing submitted POs retroactively route to them as the approver, or does the role change only affect future submissions?

When I set the Manager field on a user's profile, how does PO.vin handle it if that manager is later deactivated — do the deactivated manager's reports automatically re-route, or does their queue go dark?
Can I export a complete list of all users, their roles, and their manager assignments from Manage Hub Users, or is the user list only visible in the UI with no export option?
The Admin Dashboard shows SLA Compliance as a stat tile — what is the SLA target, and where is it configured? Can I set the SLA threshold myself or is it hardcoded?
When I use role-preview to see the employee dashboard, can I also navigate to My Requests and see a specific employee's requests, or does role-preview show a generic empty view?
Is there a way to see a log of all admin actions — user deactivations, threshold changes, vendor edits, import commits — in one place, or do I need to look at per-PO audit trails for governance?
If a new integration key is added and immediately fails verification, is there a cooldown period before I can rotate it, or can I attempt a rotation right away?
